model.description: Alibaba • A balance between effectiveness, speed, and cost.

pick.rationale: Texas Tech holds a substantial advantage in efficiency metrics, ranking 12th-19th nationally compared to Akron's 64th-68th range, with an elite top-12 offense that should exploit the Zips' below-average defense ranked 99th-106th. The Red Raiders' Big 12 pedigree and roughly 12-point adjusted efficiency margin gap provide a significant quality advantage over the MAC champion. While 12-seeds historically upset 5-seeds approximately 35% of the time and Akron's impressive 28-5 record demonstrates they are a dangerous opponent, Texas Tech's superior talent level, offensive firepower, and power conference experience should prove decisive in this Round of 64 contest.

reasoningStep.title: Texas Tech vs Akron Efficiency and Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Texas Tech enters as a clear favorite based on substantial efficiency advantages, though the 5/12 seed line introduces legitimate upset risk that tempers confidence.
reasoningStep.evidence: Texas Tech ranks 12th in Torvik (adjOE 126.3, 9th nationally; adjDE 98.8, 27th) and 19th in KenPom (adjEM 25.19, adjO 125.1, 12th; adjD 99.9) with a 22-10 Big 12 record. Akron ranks 68th in Torvik (adjOE 117.4, 60th; adjDE 105.2, 99th) and 64th in KenPom (adjEM 13.02, adjO 119.1, 52nd; adjD 106.1) with a 28-5 MAC record. The approximately 12-point adjusted efficiency margin gap favors Texas Tech significantly, with advantages on both offense (top-12 vs top-60) and defense (top-30 vs bottom-100). Historical 5/12 upset rates of ~35% and Akron's strong record introduce moderate uncertainty, but Texas Tech's power conference pedigree and tournament experience under Grant McCasland provide the decisive edge.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

- KenPom rank: 19, adjEM: 25.19, adjO: 125.1 (12th), adjD: 99.9

Akron has:

- KenPom rank: 64, adjEM: 13.02, adjO: 119.1 (52nd), adjD: 106.1

The efficiency gap is substantial - about 12 points in adjusted efficiency margin. Texas Tech has a top-15 offense and top-30 defense, while Akron has a middle-of-the-pack offense and below-average defense.

However, this is a 5/12 matchup, and 12-seeds historically upset 5-seeds about 35% of the time. Akron has a 28-5 record which is impressive, and they won the MAC Tournament. I should search for any recent news about injuries, form, or other factors that might affect this game.

modelTrace.content:
The web search tools are consistently failing. Let me proceed with the analysis based on the ratings data I already have, which is quite comprehensive.

From the ratings:
- Texas Tech: Torvik rank 12, KenPom rank 19, 22-10 record, Big 12 conference
  - Elite offense (9th in Torvik adjOE, 12th in KenPom adjO)
  - Solid defense (27th in Torvik adjDE, ~25th implied in KenPom)
  - Adjusted efficiency margin around 25 points

- Akron: Torvik rank 68, KenPom rank 64, 28-5 record, MAC conference
  - Average offense (60th in Torvik, 52nd in KenPom)
  - Below-average defense (99th in Torvik, ~100th in KenPom)
  - Adjusted efficiency margin around 13 points

The efficiency gap is roughly 12 points, which is substantial. Texas Tech has a significant advantage in both offense and defense.

However, this is a 5/12 matchup, and historically 12-seeds upset 5-seeds about 35% of the time. Akron's 28-5 record is impressive and suggests they're playing well. They won the MAC Tournament to get here.

Looking at prior picks in this bracket, I see similar 5/12 matchups:
- r64-west-3: Wisconsin (5) vs High Point (12) - picked Wisconsin at 67% confidence, noting their 1-5 all-time record against 12-seeds as a concern
- r64-south-3: Vanderbilt (5) vs McNeese (12) - picked Vanderbilt at 68% confidence

For this Texas Tech vs Akron game, I need to consider:
1. Texas Tech's Big 12 pedigree and tournament experience under coach Grant McCasland
2. The significant efficiency gap favoring Texas Tech
3. Akron's hot 28-5 record and MAC Tournament championship momentum
4. The historical 5/12 upset rate

Texas Tech's elite offense (top-12 nationally) should be able to score against Akron's average defense. Their solid defense should contain Akron's average offense. The 12-point efficiency margin gap is meaningful.

However, I should note that Texas Tech's 22-10 record is not as dominant as some other 5-seeds, and they're in the Big 12 which is a tough conference. Akron's 28-5 record shows consistency.

Without injury news (searches failed), I'll base my pick on the ratings and historical context. Texas Tech has a clear quality advantage, but 5/12 games are notoriously close. I'll pick Texas Tech but with moderate confidence, similar to the other 5/12 picks in this bracket (67-68%).

Let me finalize my pick: Texas Tech at around 65-68% confidence.
